title: L1 Adaptive Control for UAVs
description: Deployable L1 adaptive controllers providing guaranteed transient and steady-state performance bounds for fixed-wing and multi-rotor UAVs under payload uncertainty and wind disturbances. Demonstrated on Lockheed Martin platforms.

title: Mobile Manipulation in Everyday Environments
description: Integrated perception-planning-control stack for mobile manipulators operating in unstructured indoor environments — reaching, grasping, and rearranging objects without prior scene knowledge.

title: Physics-based World Simulation
description: Full-stack ROS 2 / Gazebo simulation environment supporting ground vehicles, drones, and manipulators. Enables closed-loop policy training, sim-to-real transfer, and hardware-in-the-loop testing — all before physical deployment.

title: Certifiable Agile Aerial Autonomy
description: Formally verified control and planning stack for agile aerial robots — enabling high-speed flight and swarm coordination with provable safety guarantees. Developed by the Reliable Autonomy Research Group.

videos.caption: Agile aerial autonomy with safety certificates

title: Hand-Object Interaction Forecasting
description: Predictive models for human hand-object interactions, enabling AR coaching and robot assistance in assembly, manipulation, and everyday tasks. Forecasts future states from partial observations.

videos.caption: AR coaching via hand-object interaction forecasting

title: Humanoid Robot Learning
description: Leveraging deep reinforcement learning, we train full-body humanoid controllers that acquire agile locomotion, dexterous manipulation, and whole-body coordination — bridging the sim-to-real gap to deploy learned policies directly on physical platforms.

videos.caption: Humanoid locomotion and manipulation via reinforcement learning

title: Formal Verification for Autonomous Systems
description: Automated verification tools that generate correctness certificates for cyber-physical systems — proving that an autonomous controller satisfies safety and liveness properties across all possible environments.

title: Digital Twin for Facility Monitoring
description: Reality-capture and 4D BIM pipeline that fuses LiDAR, RGB-D, and video data into continuously updated 3D digital twins, enabling automated change detection, progress monitoring, and anomaly flagging.

title: Large-Scale Motion Capture
description: Our 8,000 cubic foot motion capture facility delivers centimeter-level accuracy, enabling research in visual autonomy, high-speed navigation, and coordination of heterogeneous ground and aerial swarms.

title: Soft & Reconfigurable Robotic Actuators
description: Pneumatic and hydraulic soft actuators that conform to irregular surfaces and safely interact with delicate or unknown objects — enabling manipulation in environments where rigid robots cannot operate.
